title: When chat shows were worth watching
gist: A brief cultural notebook recommending The Dick Cavett Show archive as a model of intelligent interviewing, alongside notes on Orhan Pamuk's Museum of Innocence in Istanbul and Richard Strauss's late-career artistic redemption.

insights.claim: The Dick Cavett Show archive is a reminder that chat-show interviews were once conducted with wit and intelligence, before the format became celebrities pushing their latest product.
insights.speaker: Paul Lay
insights.so_what: A note on the cultural decline of the interview format into product promotion.
insights.quote: a time when the chat show was about considerably more than celebrities pushing their latest product

insights.claim: Richard Strauss, morally compromised by Nazism, reached remarkable late-career artistic heights (Four Last Songs, Metamorphosen, Capriccio) born of a desire for redemption.
insights.speaker: Paul Lay
insights.so_what: Illustrates artistic achievement emerging from moral compromise and the search for redemption.
insights.quote: remarkable artistic heights born of the desire for redemption
